steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: Before the blank: “Though it was designated as mission” is a dependent clause (starts with the subordinating conjunction “though”). After the blank: “mission was actually the ninety-sixth flight...” begins the independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule for introductory dependent clauses
steps.content: An introductory dependent clause must be followed by a comma. Choice D correctly uses “STS-103, the mission was actually...” placing the required comma after the mission designation. Choice B violates this by using a period instead (creating a sentence fragment); choice C by using no punctuation (run-on); and choice A by inserting an awkward “and.” That matches choice D — D is correct.
